steps.title: Identify the central paradox
steps.content: The key signal is the contradiction: astronomers know approximately 200 quasars formed very early in the universe, yet the passage states conditions were “believed to have been ill-suited to their creation.” This paradox—facts contradicting theory—is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Match the logical inference
steps.content: D captures the logical inference from the paradox: when facts contradict our understanding, the understanding must be incomplete or wrong. The other choices address unrelated properties or specific examples instead of the core puzzle. That matches choice D — D is correct.
